data.title: Audit New Liberal Arts page (typography + color)

data.body: Review verdict: PASS. Audit doc at .lattice/notes/audits/new-liberal-arts-audit.md follows audit-prompt section 10 structure. INVERSION rule documented prominently in preamble + CRITICAL INVERSION RULE block + per-row Rationales. Verified: (1) page bg #5C1010 → house-education-deep EXACT under inversion; (2) all 6 text-white/text-white/90/color:#fff sites migrate to text-house-education-deep-foreground (the paired-foreground sibling of the {deep} page surface, resolves to cream) — NO row proposes text-house-education-light as body voice; (3) SectorHeader prop #C41E20 → house-education-light NEAR with explicit value-drift Rationale (the standout finding — #C41E20 doesn't match either canonical palette value, closest to {light}); (4) FractalPattern #B52828 site documented as EXCLUDED per FRAC-20/FRAC-24 precedent; (5) selection chrome captured with two migrations (cream-on-deep-red inversion preserved via paired-foreground tokens, with FRAC-42-canonical-chrome alternative noted); (6) PretextParagraph cluster (4 sites) classified GAP-LOG-AND-MIGRATE with sitewide-dedup pointer to existing Lab GAP entry — no new audit-gaps entries appended; (7) cross-house leak check + inversion check both documented as performed. 3 typography rows + 5 color rows total; structurally simpler than Campus (no PhotoPlaceholder, no italic asides, no button color overrides). Forward observations highlight the SectorHeader value drift as the likely follow-up palette discussion. No src/, DESIGN.md, or audit-prompt.md modifications. Audit-only baseline: pnpm typecheck passes (no new src regressions possible). Spec for FRAC-29 Apply is complete and row-by-row migratable.
